resume:
Priya Ramanathan — Junior Counsel candidate — 2 yrs PQE — Bangalore.

EXPERIENCE

2023 — present: Associate, Khaitan & Co (Bangalore office) — TMT / Data Protection / Tech contracts. Worked across SaaS contracting, DPDP Act readiness advisory, several M&A diligences on the tech side. Drafted MSAs, DPAs, SCCs, AUPs for both Indian and cross-border clients.

2022 — 2023: Associate, Trilegal (Mumbai office) — General Corporate / Tech transactions. Rotated through corporate, tech, and competition desks. Drafted shareholder agreements, supported one PE diligence.

2021 — 2022: Trainee, Trilegal.

EDUCATION: BA LLB (Hons), NLSIU Bangalore (2016-2021).

LICENSING: Enrolled with Bar Council of Karnataka, 2021. Admitted to practice in India.

LANGUAGES: English (native), Hindi (fluent), Tamil (fluent), Kannada (working).

SKILLS: Contract drafting, data protection, TMT-sector regulatory analysis, cross-border DPA work.

Note: figures and case names withheld per firm confidentiality policies.
jd: Junior Counsel (data privacy focus) — In-house at a Series-D Indian SaaS company (HQ Bangalore, customers across India, EU, US). Reports to Head of Legal. Team of 3 (Head of Legal + Senior Counsel + this role). Day-to-day: 1) Own the DPDP Act (India) implementation workstream end-to-end, including consent framework, data-principal request workflows, breach response SOP; 2) Review and negotiate DPAs / SCCs with EU enterprise customers (target turnaround 5 business days); 3) Support GDPR Art. 28 processor obligations; 4) Maintain the records of processing activities (RoPA); 5) Run quarterly privacy training for engineering. Must-have: 2-4 yrs PQE, at least 12 months meaningfully focused on data protection (DPDP / GDPR / both); experience drafting or substantively negotiating DPAs; comfort sitting in a small in-house team where you own a workstream rather than support one.

history.q: Welcome. Let's start with this: of the DPA work you've done, pick the one negotiation where you sat opposite the counterparty's counsel and walk me through it.
You said this was an EU enterprise customer for one of your Khaitan clients. Roughly when did this happen — quarter and year?
OK, Q3 2024. How long did the negotiation run, start to finish?
Got it. Of those rounds, which round did the SCC Module question come up, and what was the dispute?
Walk me through the timeline of just that Module question: when the counterparty first raised it, when you proposed your position, when it was resolved.

candidate_last_answer:
OK so the Module question came up — I want to say it was round 3, but actually, hmm, it might have been round 2 because I remember we were still on the main MSA and hadn't fully moved into the DPA exhibit yet, so it had to be round 2. The counterparty's counsel raised it on a call. The call was... I think it was a Tuesday in early September 2024. Or it might have been late August, because I remember our partner was on leave that week and I had to handle the call alone, and her leave was the last week of August.

So say late August 2024. They raised that since the customer was acting as a controller and our client was processing on their behalf, Module 2 (controller-to-processor) was the right pick. Our client's position internally — and I had taken this from a memo our partner had circulated maybe two months earlier, so around June 2024 — was that for this specific data flow there was actually a sub-set of data where our client was a joint controller, not a processor, and so Module 1 (controller-to-controller) needed to apply for that subset. So the dispute became: one DPA with one Module, or one DPA layering both Modules.

I sent my written position to the counterparty on... let me think... it would have been about a week after the call, so first week of September. They came back two weeks later, which puts us in mid-September, with a counter-proposal that said they would accept the layered Module structure if we agreed to a tighter sub-processor flow-down clause. That part I actually escalated to our partner who was back from leave by then, and she signed off on the trade.

Resolved final position: layered Modules (1 for the joint-controller subset, 2 for the processor-portion), tighter sub-processor consent (case-by-case, not generic), signed... I want to say end of September but it might have spilled into early October because there was a separate redlining round on Annex II that took an extra week.

Sorry, I realize I said earlier the whole negotiation was 'about 6 weeks' but laying this out the Module piece alone took 4-5 weeks, so the total was probably closer to 8 weeks. I should correct that.

top_question_traits: expected-yield: force candidate to commit to specific dates for the partner-handover moment and partner sign-off, exposing whether candidate independently authored the layered-Module position or merely transmitted partner's pre-existing memo — disentangles 'I drafted it' from 'I argued for it'
